{{Quote|That day, I was sharp. I flew circles around those TIE bastards and shot them all to hell. By the time reinforcements arrived, well … there wasn't much left.|Halley Kadorto, to Keyan Farlander|<ref name="XW:TOSG" />}}
'''Halley Kadorto''' was a veteran of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] who became a famed [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. He flew in several early missions, suffering from the mechanical problems experienced by early Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and narrowly escaping [[Death/Legends|death]] on more than one occasion. Kadorto became famous due to his part in [[Rescue at Mon Calamari|rescuing]] [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. The early arrival of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Warrior (Star Destroyer)|''Warrior'']] jeopardized the rescue, but Kadorto intercepted waves of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]], launched from the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] to kill the slaves rather than let them fall into enemy hands. He was later awarded a [[Kalidor Crescent/Legends|Kalidor Crescent]] for his actions. Kadorto retired shortly after his most famous mission, though he continued to be involved with the Alliance.

==Biography==
===Early career===
Born during the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Kadorto eventually joined the military and became a veteran of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. After the defeat of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] and the [[Declaration of a New Order]], which saw the Republic transformed into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Kadorto continued his military life, eventually becoming involved in the fledgling [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="XW:TOSG" />

As a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], Kadorto flew many missions and became an early hero of the Alliance. During the beginning of the Rebellion, [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were prone to mechanical failure in missions, something which the Alliance was eventually able to overcome, but these problems caused many fighters to be lost. Kadorto suffered from such malfunctions and was shot up several times. He would later state that he was technically dead on "three or four occasions" before being revived by a [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]].<ref name="XW:TOSG" />

===Rescue at Mon Calamari===
{{Quote|I saw the TIEs zeroing in on the distant containers and took off to see what they were up to. I wasn't following orders. Instinct, maybe. Luck. Doesn't matter much, does it?|Halley Kadorto, to Keyan Farlander|<ref name="XW:TOSG" />}}
[[File:Kadorto.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Keyan Farlander recreates Kadorto's role in the rescue at Mon Calamari.]]
Kadorto's [[Rescue at Mon Calamari|most famous mission]] occurred above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] had discovered that the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Warrior (Star Destroyer)|''Warrior'']] was to secretly rendezvous with a convoy of [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighters]] and [[Class-A cargo container|Class-A Cargo Containers]] to receive a shipment of [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] and war matériel.<ref name="XW" /> Seeing an opportunity to secure Mon Calamari support,<ref name="TFP">{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}}</ref> the fledgling Rebel Alliance quickly dispatched a combined force of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]], and [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] to rescue the slaves. Kadorto, flying as [[Red Two/Legends|Red Two]] of [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]], was to engage the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] escorting the convoy while [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron's]] Y-wings followed shortly to disable the Imperial [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. Once the ships carrying the Mon Calamari were identified, the Alliance freighters ''[[Citadel 1]]'' and ''[[Citadel 2]]'' would arrive to conduct the boarding operation. The entire operation was devised to be concluded before the ''Warrior'' arrived.<ref name="XW" />

The mission started smoothly, as Red Squadron caught the defending TIE Fighters out of position, and initially had no trouble keeping them away from the Y-wings as they disabled the Imperial freighters.<ref name="TFP" /> Kadorto quickly identified which ships were carrying the slaves, and the Alliance freighters arrived to board the now-disabled vessels. However, before they could dock, the ''Warrior'' arrived ahead of schedule and launched a counterattack consisting of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] and more TIE Fighters.<ref name="XW" /> The X-wings were still fighting the escorts and, as they now found themselves outnumbered almost three to one, it appeared that the [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]] would prove too numerous to contain.<ref name="TFP" />

[[File:Kadorto2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kadorto's approach to the ''Warrior'', as reenacted by Farlander]]
Kadorto, however, saw the incoming fighters and decided to break away to investigate what they were up to.<ref name="XW:TOSG" /> It soon became apparent that the bombers were targeting their own convoy, choosing to kill the slaves rather than risk them being freed to join the Alliance. Seeing the danger posed by the bombers, Kadorto approached the ''Warrior'' to engage the incoming TIEs.<ref name="XW" /> Though he called for assistance, his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]] were all engaged and struggling to contain the TIE Fighter escorts, forcing him to work alone.<ref name="TFP" /> Kadorto single-handedly eliminated waves of TIE bombers and fighters until the Alliance freighters completed the boarding operation and jumped to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]]. The Rebel fighters destroyed the ships containing Imperial war matériel before they too escaped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="XW" />

For his role in saving the lives of the Mon Calamari slaves, Kadorto was awarded a [[Kalidor Crescent/Legends|Kalidor Crescent]] and came to be regarded as one of the Alliance's most daring X-wing pilots.<ref name="XW" /> The Mon Calamari were grateful for the rescue of their people and went on to become vital members of the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="TFP" /> The mission itself was later recreated as a [[Flight simulator/Legends|combat simulation]] for training [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilots]], with [[Cadet/Legends|cadets]] taking on the role of Kadorto.<ref name="XW" />

===Retirement===
Although Kadorto continued to serve the Alliance as a pilot for a time after the mission, he eventually retired sometime before the start of [[Operation Strike Fear]]<ref name="XW:TOSG" /> in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].<ref name="1BBY">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY.</ref> Although now a civilian, Kadorto was still involved with the Alliance and could often be found aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. It was there that he met [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] on the day that the young pilot recreated Kadorto's mission during his training, and told the rookie about his experiences. Toward the end of Operation Strike Fear, Kadorto attended a planning meeting, led by [[Commander/Legends|Commanders]] [[H. S'man|S'man]] and [[Lagrane]], during which a plan was formulated to steal an Imperial [[Warhead/Legends|warhead]] and use it to destroy the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']].<ref name="XW:TOSG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Don't let up for an instant, or you'll be staring into the ugly face of one of those medical droids—if you're lucky!|Halley Kadorto, to Keyan Farlander|<ref name="XW:TOSG" />}}
Kadorto was a skilled pilot whose superior shooting allowed him to survive against great odds at Mon Calamari.<ref name="TFP" /> Though he considered it a proud day in his life, Kadorto regarded it as no more important than any of the missions he flew before or after.<ref name="XW:TOSG" /> However, the mission earned him a reputation as a particularly daring pilot.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|191|Kadorto, Halley}}</ref> A friendly individual, Kadorto was happy to share his experiences with younger Rebels.<ref name="XW:TOSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Halley Kadorto was created for the [[1993]] [[Star Wars video games|game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], in which players can recreate his mission to Mon Calamari as a training exercise. The mission briefing gave a brief description of the mission,<ref name="XW" /> which was also mentioned in [[Rusel DeMaria]]'s accompanying novella ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'', which told the story of the player character, Keyan Farlander.<ref name="TFP" /> DeMaria later expanded on the character's story in ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''.<ref name="XW:TOSG" /> The depiction of the Mon Calamari mission that appeared in ''The Farlander Papers'' differs from the events as described in ''Star Wars: X-wing'', making no mention of the ''Warrior''{{'s}} arrival and stating that Kadorto pursued a group of TIE Fighters that broke off from the main escort pack to attack the freighters.<ref name="XW" /><ref name="TFP" />

''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' established that Kadorto was a veteran of the Clone Wars but did not specify on which side of the conflict he fought. The book also established that Kadorto retired from duty before meeting Farlander. Since the X-wing starfighter he flew in his most famous mission had only been in service for a matter of months at that point, Kadorto appears to have retired shortly after receiving his Kalidor Crescent.<ref name="XW:TOSG" /><ref name="XW" />
